rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 8/10 and rest on the same pillars: extensive software development and leadership experience, open-source strength, missing UML, and German proficiency below the C1 requirement. The shuffled wording (IT consulting vs. IT security/risk, Python mention) is ordinary run-to-run variation, not a substantive shift. Crucially, neither evaluation references the caregiving career gap or its length, so nothing keys off the changed demographic detail.
